title: crotalaria duboisii subsp. duboisii
intro: Crotalaria duboisii subsp. duboisii, also known as the 'Uvira rattle box' or 'Uvira rattle bush', is a plant species from the Fabaceae family, native to tropical Africa. It has been found to possess medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which have been used to treat various health conditions. The health benefits of Crotalaria duboisii subsp. duboisii include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and treat respiratory infections. In traditional medicine, the plant is prepared by boiling its leaves or roots to make a tea, which is then consumed to harness its medicinal benefits.
